description_long: This is a medium-sized, irregularly shaped stone with a rugged, angular form and a coarse, granular surface. Its coloration is predominantly a mix of dark gray and black, interspersed with prominent reddish-brown patches and lighter speckles, giving it a mottled, natural appearance. The stone's surface is uneven, with visible protrusions and indentations, suggesting it has not been artificially shaped or polished. One side features a lighter, almost rectangular patch, possibly a remnant of a label or a naturally occurring mineral inclusion. The overall texture appears hard and rough, with a matte finish that diffuses light rather than reflecting it. This stone would be commonly found in urban parks, gardens, or as part of landscaping features, serving as a natural obstacle or decorative element.
description: A rugged, irregular stone with a coarse, mottled surface of dark gray, black, and reddish-brown patches. Its hard, rough texture and natural appearance make it suitable for urban landscaping or as an obstacle.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its mottled dark gray and reddish-brown coloration and rough, uneven surface.
description_view_1: This angle reveals a more triangular profile, emphasizing the stone's angular edges and the distribution of reddish-brown patches.
description_view_2: This side displays a prominent lighter rectangular patch, contrasting with the surrounding dark and speckled surface.
description_view_3: This perspective shows a more compact, blocky aspect of the stone, with less visible color variation and a focus on its rough texture.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or obstacle on the ground
attribute_pattern: Mottled pattern with irregular reddish-brown patches and lighter speckles on a dark gray base; no artificial carvings or regularity.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with minor surface pitting and no significant signs of lichen or deep erosion, indicating moderate natural weathering.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground cover or other landscaping stones, providing a naturalistic contrast to paved or manicured urban environments.
